meta.attribution: Brodaric & Richard 2021, GSC Open File 8796, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Anticline.definition_en_canonical: A fold whose core contains the stratigraphically older rocks
gso:Anticline.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Antiform.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Chevron_Fold.definition_en_canonical: A fold with straight limbs and sharp hinges. Well developed, these folds develop repeated set of v-shaped beds.  Inter-limb angles are generally 60 degrees or less. Chevron folding preferentially occurs in well bedded units where  bedding regularly alternates between contrasting competences.
gso:Chevron_Fold.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold.definition_en_canonical: A curve or bend of a planar structure, such as rock strata, bedding planes, foliation or cleavage. A fold is usually the product of deformation, involving the compression of strata, but may include primary structures, as its definition is descriptive, not genetic. (Neuendorf et al., 2011).
gso:Fold.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold_Amplitude.definition_en_canonical: the length of half the distance from the crest of a fold to the adjacent troughs.
gso:Fold_Amplitude.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold_Axial_Surface.definition_en_canonical: Abstract surface that bisects the limbs of a fold or is the normal to a surface defined by the poles to the folded surface (typically visualized on a stereonet).  The axial surface of a particular fold may be located based on observations of the folded geologic structure, but in general it has no direct physical manifestation. Contrast with hinge surface, which is defined as the surface containing a set of hinge lines in the stack of folded surfaces defining a fold structure.  As a geologic surface, the axial surface has geometric properties, including orientation, which may be specified by observations at one or more locations, or generalized using terminology (upright, inclined, reclined, recumbent, overturned). (revised from GeoSciML 3.2)
gso:Fold_Axial_Surface.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold_Hinge.definition_en_canonical: the line linking points of maximum curvature on a folded surface (http://www.geosci.usyd.edu.au/users/prey/Teaching/Geos-2123/Folds/Sld22.html). A low-dimensional feature
gso:Fold_Hinge.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold_Hinge_Surface.definition_en_canonical: Surface that contains the hinge lines in a stack of folded layers
gso:Fold_Hinge_Surface.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold_InterLimb_Angle.definition_en_canonical: The angle between adjacent limbs of a fold, normally determined by drawing tangents to the fold limbs at the inflection points.
gso:Fold_InterLimb_Angle.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold_Limb.definition_en_canonical: The generally planar region of a fold which lies between two adjacent hinge lines and is confined between the zones of maximum curvature. A role played by some layered rock reated to the adjacent fold hinges.  Not necessarily a feature
gso:Fold_Limb.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold_Limb_Shape.definition_en_canonical: Straight vs curved limbs, eg kink, chevron, sinusoidal, box
gso:Fold_Limb_Shape.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold_Span.definition_en_canonical: linear distance between inflection points in a single fold (GeoSciML v3.2)
gso:Fold_Span.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold_Symmetry.definition_en_canonical: Concordance or discordance of bisecting surface and axial surface, or ratio of length of limbs. Folded surface may have asymmetry defined by limb length ratio if inflection points are defined. Definition based on bisecting surface/axial surface angle depends on having multiple surfaces defined such that the axial surface may be identified. (symmetric, assymetric)
gso:Fold_Symmetry.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold_System.definition_en_canonical: A collection of congruent folds  (axis and axial surface are parallel) produced by the same tectonic event. Sometimes referred to as a \
gso:Fold_System.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Fold_Wavelength.definition_en_canonical: The distance between adjacent crest or trough of  a single fold, or the average distance between adjacent crests or troughs in a fold system.
gso:Fold_Wavelength.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Hinge_Line_Curvature.definition_en_canonical: Variation in orientation of fold hinge along trend of fold, distinguish sheath from cylindrical folds. Specify with terminology eg sheath, dome, basin, cylindrical.
gso:Hinge_Line_Curvature.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Hinge_Shape.definition_en_canonical: Rounded vs. angular hinge zones; has to do with the proportion of the wavelength that is considered part of hinge
gso:Hinge_Shape.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Is_Periodic.definition_en_canonical: boolean value, true if wavelength of folds in a fold system is approximately the same
gso:Is_Periodic.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Monocline.definition_en_canonical: step-like fold in rock strata consisting of a zone of steeper dip within an otherwise horizontal or gently-dipping sequence.
gso:Monocline.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Neutral_Fold.definition_en_canonical: A fold which closes laterally and is therefore neither antiformal nor synformal.
gso:Neutral_Fold.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Ptygmatic_Fold.definition_en_canonical: Chaotic and highly sinuous fold typically in an isolated layer within a more homogeneous rock body. Typical of sedimentary slump folding and veins or dikes in medium to high-grade metamorphic rocks
gso:Ptygmatic_Fold.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Recumbent.definition_en_canonical: Surface dips between 0 and 10 degrees; typically used to describe orientation of fold hing surface or axial surface, use equivalent term 'Horizontal inclination' for other geologic surfaces.
gso:Recumbent.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Recumbent_Fold.definition_en_canonical: A fold that has an essentially horizontal axial plane.
gso:Recumbent_Fold.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Syncline.definition_en_canonical: A fold of which the core contains the stratigraphically younger rocks
gso:Syncline.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Synform.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Upright_Inclination.definition_en_canonical: Surface dips between 80 and 90 degrees, used to describe orientation of fold hinge surface or axial surface;  use equivalent term 'Vertical inclination' for other geologic surfaces.
gso:Upright_Inclination.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:Vertical_Fold.definition_en_canonical: A fold for which the fold axis is vertical and axial surface is upright.
gso:Vertical_Fold.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296
